evals.name: deploy-invoke-contract
evals.prompt: I have a Soroban contract built at target/wasm32v1-none/release/mycounter.wasm. I want to deploy it to testnet under the alias "counter", funded by my "alice" identity, and then call its increment function. Give me the exact stellar CLI commands.
evals.expected_output: A sequence of stellar CLI commands using `stellar contract deploy --wasm ... --source alice --network testnet --alias counter` followed by `stellar contract invoke --id counter --source alice --network testnet -- increment`. Should reference the slop (`--`) for the invoke args.

evals.name: multisig-payment-tx
evals.prompt: Using the stellar CLI, build a transaction that sends 50 XLM from alice to GBDEST..., then add a second payment of 10 USDC (issuer GABC...) from alice to GBOTHER..., sign it with both alice and bob, and submit it to testnet. Show the piped commands.
evals.expected_output: A piped sequence using `stellar tx new payment --build-only` -> `stellar tx op add payment` (with --asset USDC:GABC...) -> `stellar tx sign --sign-with-key alice` -> `stellar tx sign --sign-with-key bob` -> `stellar tx send`. Should use --build-only on the first step and pipe XDR between commands.

evals.name: read-only-invoke-and-storage
evals.prompt: I have a deployed contract aliased "vault" on testnet. I want to (a) call its get_balance function as a read-only view without submitting a transaction, and (b) read the raw persistent storage entry keyed by the symbol "Balance" from that contract. What are the stellar CLI commands?
evals.expected_output: Uses `stellar contract invoke --id vault --network testnet --send no -- get_balance` (with --send no BEFORE the -- slop) for the read-only call, and `stellar contract read --id vault --key Balance --durability persistent --output json` for the storage read. Must not put --send inside the slop.
